pi_items.source: Medical literature supports airway dimensions as low as 2-3mm in severe obstruction; DICOM resolution 0.4mm allows measurement of 2mm objects (~5 pixels)
Normal upper pharyngeal airway 15-20mm; 25mm is above normal but plausible for certain anatomical variations
Medical literature confirms PAS (Posterior Airway Space) <11mm indicates OSA risk; see https://ajronline.org/doi/pdf/10.2214/ajr.140.3.543
Intermediate range between normal (>11mm) and severely narrowed (<6mm); consistent with clinical airway assessment protocols
Severe airway obstruction threshold; critical narrowing in medical literature
DICOM metadata: PatientName field - indicates synthetic phantom data
DICOM metadata: PixelSpacing [0.4, 0.4] - high resolution for dental CBCT per https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4614158/
DICOM metadata: SliceThickness 0.4 - isotropic voxel suitable for airway measurement
DICOM directory contains slice_0000.dcm through slice_0099.dcm (100 total slices)
Calculated from pixel count and spacing

pi_items.verified_value: These scripts do not exist in the task directory

privileged_info_summary: The analyze_airway_patency task uses synthetic dental CBCT phantom data (DICOM format, 100 slices, 0.4mm isotropic resolution) to simulate pharyngeal airway analysis in Blue Sky Plan software. The data is confirmed synthetic with PatientName 'DENTAL^PHANTOM' and PatientID 'BSP_TEST_001'. The task classification thresholds (Normal >11mm, Narrowed 6-11mm, Severely narrowed <6mm) are based on real medical standards for Posterior Airway Space measurement used in sleep apnea screening. The plausible measurement range (2.0-25.0mm) is appropriate for oropharyngeal airway dimensions given the DICOM resolution. However, the referenced .bsp project file does not exist on the filesystem, and PowerShell setup/export scripts referenced in the task hooks are missing from the task directory.
